summary: The patient exhibits hallmark signs of primary Sjögren’s syndrome—persistent xerostomia and xerophthalmia—with high titers of anti-Ro/anti-La antibodies confirming diagnosis. This condition significantly elevates the risk of developing non-Hodgkin lymphoma, making it the most clinically significant malignant consequence among listed options.

This condition significantly elevates the risk of developing non-Hodgkin lymphoma, making it the most clinically significant malignant consequence among listed options.. Answer choice: B
